parentQuestion: The serous membrane covering the abdominal organs is called ___.

options: Thin, slippery membranes that line body cavities and produce watery fluid
scaffolds.explain: SEROUS MEMBRANES are THIN, SLIPPERY membranes that line body cavities. They produce watery fluid that reduces friction.

wrongExplains.0: Serous membranes are thin and slippery, not thick and rough.
wrongExplains.2: Serous membranes line thoracic and abdominal cavities.
wrongExplains.3: Serous membranes are found in body cavities, not muscles.

scaffolds.q: How many layers do serous membranes have?
scaffolds.explain: Serous membranes have TWO LAYERS: PARIETAL (lines wall) and VISCERAL (covers organ). Two sides of the same membrane.

wrongExplains.0: Serous membranes have two distinct layers.
wrongExplains.2: Serous membranes have two layers, not three.

scaffolds.q: What does the parietal layer of a serous membrane line?
scaffolds.explain: PARIETAL layer lines the WALL of the cavity. Parietal = wall. Think of it as wallpaper lining the cavity.

wrongExplains.0: Visceral layer covers organs; parietal lines walls.
wrongExplains.2: Parietal lines cavity walls, not blood vessels specifically.
wrongExplains.3: Serous membranes line cavities, not bones.

scaffolds.q: What does the visceral layer of a serous membrane cover?
scaffolds.explain: VISCERAL layer covers the ORGANS themselves. Visceral = organs. Like shrink-wrap on the organ surface.

wrongExplains.0: Parietal lines walls; visceral covers organs.
wrongExplains.2: Visceral covers entire organs, not just blood vessels.
wrongExplains.3: Visceral covers entire organs, not just nerves.

scaffolds.q: What is the name of the serous membrane in the abdominal cavity?
scaffolds.explain: The PERITONEUM is the serous membrane of the abdominal cavity. Pleura = lungs. Pericardium = heart. Peritoneum = abdomen.

wrongExplains.0: Pleura surrounds lungs; peritoneum is abdominal.
wrongExplains.2: Pericardium surrounds heart; peritoneum is abdominal.
wrongExplains.3: Meninges surround brain/spinal cord; peritoneum is abdominal.

scaffolds.explain: VISCERAL PERITONEUM covers the abdominal ORGANS — the layer directly touching stomach, intestines, liver, etc.

wrongExplains.0: Parietal peritoneum lines the wall; visceral covers organs.
wrongExplains.2: Visceral pleura covers lungs; visceral peritoneum covers abdominal organs.
wrongExplains.3: Visceral pericardium covers heart; visceral peritoneum covers abdominal organs.

scaffolds.explain: PARIETAL PERITONEUM lines the abdominal WALL (not the organs) — the layer touching the inside of your abdominal wall.

wrongExplains.0: Visceral peritoneum covers organs; parietal lines walls.
wrongExplains.2: Parietal pleura lines chest wall; parietal peritoneum lines abdominal wall.
wrongExplains.3: Parietal pericardium lines heart sac; parietal peritoneum lines abdominal wall.

scaffolds.q: Which membrane covers abdominal organs specifically?
scaffolds.explain: VISCERAL PERITONEUM covers abdominal organs. Visceral = organ-covering. Peritoneum = abdominal membrane.

wrongExplains.0: Parietal peritoneum lines walls, not organs.
wrongExplains.2: Visceral pleura covers lungs, not abdominal organs.
wrongExplains.3: Parietal pericardium is part of heart sac, not abdominal.

scaffolds.q: The serous membrane covering the abdominal organs is called ___?
scaffolds.explain: The answer is VISCERAL PERITONEUM — the serous membrane covering abdominal organs. Not parietal (that's the wall), not mesentery (that's a double layer connecting organs).

wrongExplains.0: Parietal peritoneum lines the wall, not covers organs.
wrongExplains.2: Mesentery is a double-layer extension, not the organ covering itself.
wrongExplains.3: Parietal pleura is in the chest, not abdomen.
